Staff of the Morning Dew  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Blunt Atk Delay: 12
DMG: 4 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STA: +10 INT: +25 MANA: +100
SV FIRE: +10 SV DISEASE: +10 SV COLD: +10 SV MAGIC: +10 SV POISON: +10
WT: 3.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: HUM ERU HIE DEF GNM IKS FRG DRK
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

This item is the result of a quest.

Quest Name

Drops

NPC Name

anyways, why flame all the damn time , JESUS CHRIST!...back to da point... this item is very nice, +25INT, +100mana... hell i like it, if ya come across this congratulate yourself on making it that far in the game, not going, "well my epic plus "this shield" is better but the ratio" who cares, it a nice item and if you have come that far you worked your *** off and earned it.

Hehe. What do you think they were talking about when they named this item Morning Dew. Sounds like it should drop off of a sexy female. Just thought I'd throw in a little humor amongst all this flame.
▲ Page top
its not that bad
QuoteReply
# Jan 16 2003 at 12:36 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591778486992
10 posts
Buddy of mine just got this staff (he is 62 necro), but really the only reason he got it was because he was one of 2 casters in ST when it dropped and the other caster didn't want it so he took it. He has epic, zheart, and this staff. That he can equipt in his primary, and he has Shield of Infused Power in secondary (For those of u unfamiliar with shield of infused power, it is one of the better caster shields in game, offering a ton of stats, namely 25 magic resist, look it up.)

This staff offers him 5 more int and 20 more mana than his epic does. But its not really a huge difference in his personal stats as opposed to epic or zheart. Its a good replacement if you don't have either. Of course 9 times out of 10, if your a caster and you have ST key, more than likely you have zheart since zlandicar is one of the dragons that drops st key, but hey, look at it this way, now you can sell that zheart for 30k.)

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 11 2002 at 4:23 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Ok, now I have been playing EQ since the beta testing its self. When epics came out I damned near nut all over myself. Epics are with out a doubt in my mind very powerful weapons, and very useful weapons. I also know that some of the epics, example: Necromancers, SUCK CACK doing and take many many many months. Most necromancers spend about 7 months on their epic.

▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 21 2001 at 4:34 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) look closely at the stats, there are little patch marks/boxes where all the stats appear, all very blurred and smudged, i have never said this about any item before, but i truly think this is fake, feel free to contradict me, peace.
RE: fake =-/
QuoteReply
# Aug 14 2001 at 2:57 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965864169
76 posts
The smudges and blurs you see are actually jpeg compression. JPEG uses 8x8 blocks and attempts to determine ways to save that block using fewer bytes, but the result is this noise pattern you see, which is always most obvious around high-contrast areas (i.e. where the very dark black text meets the relatively light tan). The higher your compression ratio, the more obvious this noise becomes, but the smaller the file size.

This is an amazingly impressive item to many, though I have a feeling that at level 55+ this is simply an impressive item, as these are probably individuals who have a lot of their stats maxed already. Not that unrealistic for dropping in the sleeper's tomb, possibly one of the most dangerous areas in the game.
▲ Page top
RE: fake =-/
QuoteReply

miss
miss
miss

miss

you obviously havent seen my wiz melee...
miss
-take 40
hit for 3

-hit for 30

-hit for 45
dead
CONGRAGULUTIONS, YOU ARE NOW LEVEL 12!
▲ Page top
RE: question sir
QuoteReply
# Nov 20 2002 at 12:22 AM Rating: Decent
LifegiverOfXegony
Scholar
105 posts
By the time your wizard is high enough to actually possess this item, his melee will look something like this:

You try to hit random_mob01 but miss!
Random_mob01 hits YOU for 199 points of damage!
Random_mob01 hits YOU for 190 points of damage!
Random_mob01 tries to kick YOU but misses!
You try to hit random_mob01 but miss!
Random_mob01 hits YOU for 182 points of damage!
Random_mob01 hits YOU for 160 points of damage!
You try to hit random_mob01 but miss!
You try to hit random_mob01 but miss!
Random_mob01 hits YOU for 199 points of damage!
Random_mob01 hits YOU for 199 points of damage!
Random_mob01 kicks YOU for 68 points of damage!
You have been slain by random_mob01!

Insert 2 or 3 rounds of melee with damage absorb messages before you start taking damage, if you've clicked your epic first or casted one of the damage absorbing skin spells.

If you're in a raid then your 12 delay weapon will probably get you killed on ripostes before you hit the mob for a single point of damage, particularly if it enrages.
____________________________
Philip <Chef's Kiss>, Teek server, 70 monk
Axcellent <Chill Mode>, Vaniki server, 115 zerker
{retired} Rangerer <Scions of Time>, Agnarr server, 65 ranger
{retired} Lifegiver <Iratus Lepus>, 100 Druid, Xegony

I would be interested in trying this with my Vampiric Embrace on. I think it's procing for 68 now. since Vampiric Embrace procs even with misses....

dark
pet
fear
melee

proc - get 68 health back
fear wears off
get hit for 117
get hit for 117
bashed for 25
stunned

# Apr 15 2001 at 3:41 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361254562
View User Forum
119 posts
I was going to say pretty much exactly what Pene did before I read his. Moss Covered Twig was over powered in meleer's hands with much higher skill caps and ability to take damage (cause in primary hand it taunted like a SOB).

So the only good thing about it, will allow casters to max their 2 hb on easy low blues when group is allowing them to melee. Woot, big woop, he/she might do 50 or 100 dmg, probably not even that much.

Sad thing is, like too many other caster items in particular found in places such as Sleeper's, anyone able to enter VP/Sleeper's has a very high chance of already obtaining their epics, and for almost anyone who does have epic, no way would they loose it and off hand shield item for this.

*sarcasm on*
Good for those 4 peeps able to reach this zone and kill these most uber mobs, but don't have their epic.
*sarcasm off*

# Apr 11 2001 at 9:21 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593971708573

194 posts
I have no clue whether or not this item exists or not. I will probably NEVER see Sleeper's tomb because I cannot commit all my free time to Everquest (besides, I plan on jumping on Star Wars Galaxies as soon as it comes out.) But the reason I think people are saying this is fake is because of the uglyness of the screen shot. The words are running over the side, and the text has "noise" around them. It could be real, but +25 INT seems like a lot for a weapon.

just my 2cp worth.
▲ Page top
RE: Hear me out
QuoteReply
# May 13 2001 at 8:21 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591754966424
Sage
119 posts
The noise is part of the lossy compression of the JPEG graphic format; the person who made the picture decided to choose a lower image quality, making the picture smaller (in terms of file size) at the expense of lost picture information. Notice that the noise occurs in small square blocks--part of how the picture is divided up for encoding.

It also looks like the person may have converted to JPEG and _then_ scaled the image, so the noise is even more visible--the noise is introduced then scaled rather having noise introduced into an already scaled picture.

As far as words running over the side, this is what Everquest does when it has a lot of stats to display.

Also keep in mind that this is a two handed weapon. Yes it gives +25 int (along with mana+100 and +10 to all resists), but the stats come from two slots--primary melee AND secondary melee--so combined with the difficulty of getting to the sleeper's tomb, I think it's an appropriate set of bonuses. That the staff can be used as a weapon should be more or less irrelevant, aside from killing greens and building up a rather useless skill quickly.

That said, I don't have any firsthand evidence of it...but I don't have any reason to doubt its existence either.
